metadata.title: FERPA Legal Corpus for PRAMS
metadata.description: Curated authoritative sources on FERPA education records, violations, and enforcement. Court holdings are manually curated; SPPO letters are fact-pattern references, not binding precedent.
metadata.disclaimer: This corpus supports compliance analysis; it is not legal advice. SPPO finding letters are redacted, non-precedential, and may not reflect current policy. Consult institutional counsel for contested questions.
sources: U.S. Supreme Court and federal circuit opinions
U.S. Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office (SPPO) letters

education_record_test.statutory_definition: 34 CFR § 99.3 — records directly related to a student and maintained by an educational agency or institution

elements.description: Information must concern a specific, identifiable student (not merely a name in isolation per Gonzaga context)
Information must be kept or preserved by the institution in a centralized or institutional record-keeping system (Owasso)
Includes direct identifiers and indirect identifiers that allow a reasonable person to identify the student (34 CFR § 99.3)

exclusions: Sole possession records (instructor's own memory aids, not accessible to others)
Law enforcement unit records (under certain conditions)
Employment records (when employment is not contingent on student status)
Peer-graded papers before collection by teacher (Owasso; codified in 2008 regulations)

violation_framework.regulatory_test: An educational agency or institution has a policy or practice of permitting the improper disclosure of personally identifiable information from education records without an applicable exception or consent (20 U.S.C. § 1232g(b); 34 CFR §§ 99.30–99.33).
violation_framework.enforcement: SPPO complaint investigation; possible withdrawal of federal funding for systemic noncompliance. No private right of action under § 1983 for nondisclosure (Gonzaga).
common_violation_categories: Failure to provide eligible student/parent access to education records
Improper redisclosure by school officials or contractors
Directory information released without opportunity to opt out
Disclosure without signed consent where no exception applies

entries.citation: Bauer v. Kincaid, 759 F. Supp. 575 (W.D. Mo. 1991)
entries.title: Campus security incident reports vs. education records
entries.holding: Criminal investigation and incident reports prepared by campus security are not 'education records' under FERPA when they do not meet the education-record definition.

entries.title: What definitions apply to these regulations?
entries.holding: Defines 'education record' as records directly related to a student and maintained by the institution; defines personally identifiable information and directory information.

entries.title: Under what conditions is prior consent not required to disclose?
entries.holding: Lists exceptions to prior written consent, including disclosures to school officials with legitimate educational interest and to contractors performing institutional services.

entries.title: Under what conditions is prior consent not required to disclose?
entries.holding: Enumerates permitted disclosures including to other school officials, contractors, and in health/safety emergencies; contractors must be under direct control regarding PII use.

entries.title: What recordkeeping requirements exist concerning requests and disclosures?
entries.holding: Requires institutions to maintain a record of each request and disclosure of education records, except certain school-official and directory-information disclosures.

entries.title: What limitations apply to the redisclosure of information?
entries.holding: Recipients of education records may redisclose only on conditions specified in the regulations; redisclosure must be for the same purpose.

entries.title: The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA): Legal Issues
entries.holding: Comprehensive congressional research summary of FERPA statutory framework, key cases (Owasso, Gonzaga, Miami Univ.), enforcement, and interaction with other laws.

entries.title: No private § 1983 enforcement of FERPA nondisclosure
entries.holding: FERPA's nondisclosure provisions do not create individually enforceable rights under 42 U.S.C. § 1983; students cannot sue for damages for alleged FERPA violations.

entries.citation: Hardin County Schools v. Foster, 727 F. App'x 230 (6th Cir. 2018)
entries.title: Student records in litigation context
entries.holding: Student education records remain protected under FERPA in litigation and discovery disputes; courts analyze whether records meet the education-record definition.

entries.citation: Jensen v. Reeves, 3 Fed. Appx. 905 (10th Cir. 2001)
entries.title: Limited disclosure to interested parties
entries.holding: Limited disclosure to interested parties about a child's school misbehavior may be legitimate under FERPA in certain contexts.

entries.citation: United States v. Miami Univ., 294 F.3d 797 (6th Cir. 2002)
entries.title: Student disciplinary records are education records
entries.holding: Unredacted student disciplinary records maintained by the university are 'education records' under FERPA; newspapers do not have unrestricted access.

entries.citation: Owasso Indep. Sch. Dist. No. I-011 v. Falvo, 534 U.S. 426 (2002)
entries.title: Peer grading and the 'maintained' requirement
entries.holding: Grades on peer-graded papers before the teacher collects and records them are not 'maintained' education records under FERPA because they are not kept in institutional custody.

entries.title: Anonymous/ungraded student work may be processed without participant linkage
entries.holding: Research protocol responses stored with session_id and no participant FK are not maintained as identifiable education records.

entries.title: When uncertain, assume FERPA applies
entries.holding: Ambiguous data handling defaults to FERPA-protected treatment requiring de-identification or human review.

entries.title: Do not combine student name with academic context in external AI without controls
entries.holding: Prompts containing student names plus academic context (grades, enrollment, assessments) must be screened or redacted before transmission to external AI providers.

entries.title: FERPA does not apply until student in attendance
entries.holding: Information submitted by applicants is not subject to FERPA until the applicant becomes a student in attendance at the postsecondary institution.

entries.citation: Letter of Finding Regarding Attorney Client Privilege (June 2019)
entries.title: Denial of records subject to attorney-client privilege
entries.holding: University did not violate FERPA when it properly denied education records subject to attorney-client privilege after meeting applicable criteria.

entries.title: SPPO Findings Letters Archive
entries.holding: SPPO archive index — download ZIP/PDF letters for manual curation.

entries.title: SPPO Historical Archive of Issued Letters
entries.holding: SPPO archive index — download ZIP/PDF letters for manual curation.

entries.title: Email messages as education records
entries.holding: Handwritten, typed, or computer-generated notes including email messages that identify a student and are maintained by the school constitute education records under FERPA.

entries.title: SPPO Letters of Importance
entries.holding: SPPO archive index — download ZIP/PDF letters for manual curation.

entries.citation: Letter of Finding to Middleton Cross Plains (April 2025)
entries.title: Email access dispute — insufficient evidence of violation
entries.holding: SPPO found insufficient evidence that denying parent access to certain email communications violated § 99.10; Department has not adopted Tulare holding that all emails are education records.

entries.citation: Letter to Rhode Island Board of Governors for Higher Education
entries.title: FERPA vs. state public records laws
entries.holding: Clarifies interaction between FERPA and state access-to-public-records laws; FERPA limits disclosure of education records even when state law might otherwise permit access.

entries.title: Contractors and school officials with legitimate educational interest
entries.holding: Outside parties performing institutional services may access education records if they meet school-official criteria and remain under direct control of the institution regarding PII use.

entries.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
entries.holding: Conditions federal education funding on protecting the privacy of education records; grants parents/eligible students access and amendment rights; restricts disclosure without consent.
